title: Japarandiba longifolia
taxonomy: Japarandiba longifolia is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Aurantioideae, which is where Japarandiba longifolia is classified. Within the subfamily Aurantioideae, Japarandiba longifolia is part of the tribe Citreae. The tribe Citreae is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Japarandiba longifolia's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rutaceae family.
uses: Japarandiba longifolia, also known as the Brazil nut tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's seeds, leaves, and bark are utilized to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The leaves of Japarandiba longifolia are also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. In addition, the plant's roots are used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Japarandiba longifolia's medicinal uses.
